name: Australia

outbound_official.notes: Australia's DFAT publishes 'Visas for Australians travelling overseas' at dfat.gov.au/travel/visas/visas-for-australians-travelling-overseas. However, this page is advisory/explanatory in nature - it explicitly states 'The Australian government doesn't issue visas for overseas travel and can't provide specific information on foreign visas' and directs citizens to contact each destination country's embassy or consulate. Australia does NOT publish a consolidated outbound visa-free list analogous to India's MEA VFFIN page. Per-destination entry requirements (including visa-free status for Australian passport holders) are published on individual Smartraveller country pages at smartraveller.gov.au/destinations - but no single master enumerated list exists. Rely on destination-side data and per-country Smartraveller advisories for Australian outbound access levels.

visa_free.notes: New Zealand citizens do not require a visa before arrival. They are granted a Special Category Visa (subclass 444) on arrival, which allows indefinite stay as long as they remain NZ citizens. Under the Trans-Tasman Travel Arrangement. The SCV is a temporary visa (ceases on departure) but is re-granted on each re-entry. Holders do not have the same rights as Australian permanent residents or citizens.

e_visa.notes: eVisitor (subclass 651) - free online application, multiple entries within 12-month validity, max 3 months per visit. ETA (subclass 601) is also technically available (AUD 20 fee via app) but eVisitor is the recommended/free option for European passport holders.

eVisitor (subclass 651) - eligible for United Kingdom British Citizen passport holders only. Free online application, multiple entries within 12-month validity, max 3 months per visit. British National Overseas (BNO), British Dependent Territories Citizen, British Overseas Citizen, British Protected Person and British Subject passport holders are NOT eligible for eVisitor but BNO holders ARE eligible for ETA (subclass 601, AUD 20 fee).

eVisitor (subclass 651) - passport must indicate that holder is a national of the Vatican City (Holy See). Free online application, multiple entries within 12-month validity, max 3 months per visit. ETA (subclass 601) also available (AUD 20 fee via app) but eVisitor is the free/recommended option.

eta.notes: ETA subclass 601 - applied via the Australian ETA app (AUD 20 service fee). Multiple entries within 12-month validity (or passport validity if shorter), max 3 months per visit. Ordinary passports only; non-citizen passports, certificates of identity, and Titre de Voyage are ineligible.
ETA subclass 601 - applied via the Australian ETA app (AUD 20 service fee). Multiple entries within 12-month validity (or passport validity if shorter), max 3 months per visit.

ETA subclass 601 - applied via the Australian ETA app (AUD 20 service fee). Must hold a Hong Kong SAR (HKSAR) passport. Multiple entries within 12-month validity (or passport validity if shorter), max 3 months per visit.
ETA subclass 601 - applied via the Australian ETA app (AUD 20 service fee). Multiple entries within 12-month validity (or passport validity if shorter), max 3 months per visit.
ETA subclass 601 - applied via the Australian ETA app (AUD 20 service fee). Multiple entries within 12-month validity (or passport validity if shorter), max 3 months per visit.
ETA subclass 601 - applied via the Australian ETA app (AUD 20 service fee). Multiple entries within 12-month validity (or passport validity if shorter), max 3 months per visit.
ETA subclass 601 - applied via the Australian ETA app (AUD 20 service fee). Multiple entries within 12-month validity (or passport validity if shorter), max 3 months per visit.

ETA subclass 601 - applied via the Australian ETA app (AUD 20 service fee). Ordinary passports only; official and diplomatic Taiwan passports are NOT eligible. Multiple entries within 12-month validity (or passport validity if shorter), max 3 months per visit.

ETA subclass 601 - BNO passport holders are eligible for the ETA (AUD 20 service fee via app) but NOT eligible for the eVisitor (subclass 651). Multiple entries within 12-month validity (or passport validity if shorter), max 3 months per visit.
ETA subclass 601 - applied via the Australian ETA app (AUD 20 service fee). Multiple entries within 12-month validity (or passport validity if shorter), max 3 months per visit.

visa_policy.notes: Australia does not grant visa-free entry to most nationalities - most travellers require at least an ETA or eVisitor (both pre-obtained online, not on arrival). The eVisitor (subclass 651) is free and covers European passport holders (EU member states + Andorra, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Monaco, Norway, San Marino, Switzerland, UK British Citizens, Vatican). The ETA (subclass 601) covers non-European nationalities (Brunei, Canada, Hong Kong SAR, Japan, Malaysia, Singapore, South Korea, Taiwan ordinary passports, UK BNO, USA) and carries an AUD 20 service fee via the Australian ETA app. Many European nationalities appear on the official ETA eligible list too, but eVisitor is the recommended free option. New Zealand is the only true visa-free arrangement (Trans-Tasman). All other nationalities require a Visitor Visa (subclass 600) or another applicable visa.

conditional_access.conditions: Transit without visa (TWOV) only - airside transit, must arrive by aircraft, depart to a third country by aircraft within 8 hours of arrival, must not clear immigration or leave the airport transit lounge. TWOV for diplomatic passports does NOT apply to holders of diplomatic passports from: Arab Non-National Passports, Afghanistan, Algeria, Angola, Bahrain, Comoros, Democratic People's Republic of Korea, Egypt, Iran, Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Libya, Madagascar, Mauritania, Morocco, Pakistan, Russian Federation, Saudi Arabia, Sierra Leone, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, Tunisia, Yemen and Zimbabwe - these holders must obtain an appropriate Australian visa.

Transit without visa (TWOV) only - Indian official passport holders (passports issued by the authorities of the Republic of India to officials of the Government of India) may transit Australia without a visa. Must arrive by aircraft, depart to a third country by aircraft within 8 hours of arrival, and must not clear immigration or leave the airport transit lounge.

cbi.notes: Australia does not offer a Citizenship-by-Investment program. Investment pathways lead to temporary and then permanent residency, not direct citizenship. Citizenship requires permanent residency plus a minimum 4-year stay including 1 year as a permanent resident. The Business Innovation and Investment Program (BIIP/subclass 188) closed permanently to new applications on 31 July 2024.

programs.name: Business Innovation and Investment (Provisional) visa - Investor Stream (subclass 188) [CLOSED]
programs.notes: CLOSED to new applications as of 31 July 2024. Existing applications lodged before 31 July 2024 continue to be processed. The live official page confirms minimum investment of AUD 2.5 million (updated from the previously recorded AUD 1.5 million - the official page now states 'at least AUD2.5 million'). Leads to permanent residence via subclass 888. Must be nominated by a state/territory government. Points test required.

programs.name: Business Innovation and Investment (Provisional) visa - Significant Investor Stream (subclass 188) [CLOSED]
programs.notes: CLOSED to new applications as of 31 July 2024. Existing applications lodged before 31 July 2024 continue to be processed. Required investment of at least AUD 5 million in complying significant investments under the Complying Investment Framework (CIF). Leads to permanent residence via subclass 888. No points test.

programs.name: Business Innovation and Investment (Provisional) visa - Premium Investor Stream (subclass 188) [CLOSED]
programs.notes: CLOSED to new applications as of 31 July 2024. Required investment of AUD 15 million. Only available by nomination by Austrade on behalf of the Australian government. Leads to permanent residency via subclass 888.

programs.name: Business Innovation and Investment (Provisional) visa - Business Innovation Stream (subclass 188) [CLOSED]
programs.notes: CLOSED to new applications as of 31 July 2024. For people with business skills who wanted to establish, develop and manage a new or existing business in Australia. Points test required (minimum 65 points). Must be nominated by a state/territory government or Austrade. Leads to permanent residency via subclass 888.

programs.name: Business Innovation and Investment (Permanent) visa (subclass 888)
programs.notes: Permanent residence visa for holders of subclass 188 (provisional) granted before program closure. Multiple streams: Business Innovation, Investor, Significant Investor, Entrepreneur, Premium Investor. Applicants must have held the provisional visa and continued to meet requirements. Still being processed for existing subclass 188 holders.

programs.name: National Innovation Visa (subclass 858)
programs.eligibility: Exceptionally talented individuals who have an internationally recognised record of exceptional and outstanding achievement in an eligible area. The Department must invite applicants - submission of an Expression of Interest (EOI) is required first. Eligible areas include AgriFood Tech, Space, FinTech, MedTech, CyberSecurity, Resources Technology, Quantum, and others per the current NIV priorities list.
programs.processing_time: Varies; applications processed in accordance with Skilled Visa processing priorities.
programs.notes: Permanent visa. Replaced the Global Talent Independent Program (GTI) / former Distinguished Talent Visa (858), launched 6 December 2024. Must submit an EOI; Department must invite before application. Nominator is mandatory. The salary threshold is linked to the Fair Work High Income Threshold (FWIT): from 1 July 2026 the FWIT is AUD 190,100 (increased from AUD 183,100 which applied from 1 July 2025). This figure is subject to annual indexation on 1 July each year.

programs.name: Skilled Independent visa (subclass 189) - Points-tested stream
programs.eligibility: Skilled workers with an occupation on the eligible skilled occupations list, minimum 65 points in the points test. Must submit EOI via SkillSelect and receive an invitation.
programs.processing_time: Varies; processed in accordance with annual migration planning levels and invitation rounds.
programs.notes: Permanent visa with no sponsorship or nomination required. Points assessed on age, education, English language ability, and work experience. Cannot guarantee invitation even if minimum 65 points are met.

programs.name: Business Innovation and Investment (Provisional) visa - Entrepreneur Stream (subclass 188) [CLOSED]
programs.eligibility: CLOSED to new applications as of 31 July 2024. Previously required funding agreement from a third party (e.g. venture capital) or an Entrepreneurs' Programme grant. Must be nominated by a state/territory government or Austrade.
programs.processing_time: Visa stay up to 4 years and 3 months (provisional). Existing applications only.
programs.notes: CLOSED to new applications as of 31 July 2024. Path to permanent residence via subclass 888 Entrepreneur Stream remains open for existing holders.

programs.name: Business Talent (Permanent) visa - Venture Capital Entrepreneur Stream (subclass 132) [CLOSED]
programs.eligibility: CLOSED to new applications as of 31 July 2024. Previously for people who sourced venture capital funding from a member of the Australian Investment Council (AIC) for a high-value business concept.
programs.processing_time: Permanent visa granted upon approval. Existing applications only.
programs.notes: CLOSED to new applications as of 31 July 2024. Refunds of VAC available for applicants who withdrew on or after 31 July 2024. Previously required nomination by Austrade.

validation_notes: CORRECTED (2026-06-14): ETA (subclass 601) fee updated from 'no visa application charge' to AUD 20 service fee via the Australian ETA app, confirmed from the live official page.

CORRECTED (2026-06-14): ETA eligible list expanded - the live official ETA page lists 34 passport types including many European countries (Andorra, Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Iceland, Ireland, Italy, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Malta, Monaco, Norway, Portugal, San Marino, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Netherlands, Vatican City) alongside the Asia-Pacific/Americas group. European passport holders are officially directed to use eVisitor (free) instead. ETA entries now reflect the 10 non-European/non-eVisitor-eligible nationalities plus UK BNO as ETA-only.

CORRECTED (2026-06-14): Added UK British National (Overseas) as a separate ETA entry - BNO holders ARE eligible for ETA subclass 601 (confirmed on live ETA page) but NOT eligible for eVisitor subclass 651 (confirmed on live eVisitor page). This was missing from the previous record.

CORRECTED (2026-06-14): All subclass 188 BIIP streams (Business Innovation, Investor, Significant Investor, Entrepreneur) and subclass 132 Venture Capital Entrepreneur stream marked as CLOSED. The BIIP closed permanently to new applications on 31 July 2024. Confirmed from official BIIP closure page at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/getting-a-visa/biip-closure-and-refunds.
CORRECTED (2026-06-14): Investor Stream subclass 188 minimum amount corrected from AUD 1,500,000 to AUD 2,500,000 - the live official page states 'at least AUD2.5 million'.

CORRECTED (2026-06-14): National Innovation Visa (858) salary threshold updated from AUD 175,000 to AUD 183,100 - the Fair Work High Income Threshold from 1 July 2025 is AUD 183,100 (per fairwork.gov.au library). Subject to annual indexation on 1 July.
CORRECTED (2026-06-14): Vatican City eVisitor note updated - the official eVisitor page specifies the passport must indicate that the holder is a national of the Vatican City (Holy See).
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: New Zealand SCV (subclass 444) arrangement confirmed accurate from live official page.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: TWOV (Transit-771) conditional_access entries confirmed accurate from live official page - both the diplomatic passport TWOV rule and the Indian official passport TWOV rule are correctly recorded.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: eVisitor country list confirmed accurate - all 36 entries (EU member states + Andorra, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Monaco, Norway, San Marino, Switzerland, UK British Citizens, Vatican) match the live official eVisitor page.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: Australia has no CBI program.

REVALIDATED (2026-06-27): Full re-verification run. immi.homeaffairs.gov.au returned HTTP 403 for direct page fetches; verification performed via multiple independent registered-migration-agent and official-reference sources. Findings: (1) eVisitor (651) eligible country list is unchanged at 35 nationalities - confirmed via onederland.com.au (registered migration agent) and multiple independent sources. Western Balkans countries (Serbia, Albania, Montenegro, Bosnia and Herzegovina, North Macedonia) are NOT eligible for eVisitor despite one unreliable third-party source (worldtraveldocs.com) claiming otherwise - this claim was rejected as unsupported by any official or authoritative secondary source. (2) ETA (601) list remains at 34 passport types, no new countries added. (3) NZ SCV (subclass 444) arrangement unchanged. (4) NIV 858 FWHIT remains AUD 183,100 for 2025-26 period - confirmed via multiple migration agent sources. The 1 July 2026 indexed figure is not yet confirmed (see gaps). (5) No new bilateral visa-free agreements, VoA arrangements, or conditional access changes detected. All visa_policy entries confirmed accurate; no changes made to policy data.

CORRECTED (2026-06-29): NIV 858 salary threshold updated from AUD 183,100 to AUD 190,100 effective 1 July 2026. The Fair Work High Income Threshold (FWIT) is indexed annually on 1 July; for 2026-27 it increases from AUD 183,100 to AUD 190,100. Confirmed from Addisons law firm article (addisons.com) and corroborated by Hartman Immigration and other authoritative secondary sources citing Fair Work Commission announcement. The gap item about the 1 July 2026 figure is now resolved.

REVALIDATED (2026-06-29): immi.homeaffairs.gov.au continues to return HTTP 403. Verification via Wikipedia visa policy of Australia page, ternvisa.com (updated May 2026), and multiple secondary sources confirms: (1) eVisitor (651) eligible country list unchanged at 36 entries. (2) ETA (601) eligible list unchanged at 34 passport types (9 non-European + UK BNO + 24 European countries that prefer eVisitor). (3) No new countries added to either list. (4) No new bilateral visa-free agreements or VoA arrangements. (5) ETA fee remains AUD 20; eVisitor remains free. All visa_policy and conditional_access entries confirmed accurate.
gaps: The subclass 188 investor stream minimum investment amount shown on the live official page is AUD 2.5 million, but the stream is closed to new applications so this figure is historical. The corrected amount is recorded for documentation accuracy.

National Innovation Visa (858) salary threshold is tied to the Fair Work High Income Threshold (FWIT), indexed annually on 1 July. The figure AUD 190,100 applies from 1 July 2026. The 1 July 2026 amount was confirmed from Addisons law firm and Hartman Immigration (citing Fair Work Commission), not directly from fairwork.gov.au (which returned timeout on 2026-06-29). Re-verify against fairwork.gov.au/tools-and-resources/library/K600486_High-income-threshold-amounts to confirm the official figure once the page is accessible.
The complete path-to-PR timelines for subclass 188 investment streams and subclass 888 depend on individual state/territory nomination conditions; no fixed durations are stated on official pages.
No visa-on-arrival policy exists for Australia - confirmed from official sources.

No credential-based conditional entry rules found on any official Australian government source: Australia does not grant visa-free, VoA, e-visa, or ETA access to travellers based solely on holding a valid US, UK, Schengen, Canadian, or other third-country visa or residence permit. Every traveller must independently meet Australian visa requirements.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder conditional entry rule found for Australia.

No bilateral diplomatic/official passport visa suppression treaties found. DFAT Protocol Guidelines confirm diplomatic staff posted to Australia require a formal subclass 995 diplomatic visa - diplomatic passport alone does not confer right of entry for non-transit purposes.

sources_checked: https://onederland.com.au/651-visa-evisitor/ (2026-06-27 revalidation - confirmed eVisitor 651 eligible country list unchanged)
https://www.australianvisaonline.com/skilled-migration/national-innovation-visa-subclass-858 (2026-06-27 revalidation - NIV 858 salary threshold AUD 183,100 confirmed for 2025-26)
https://www.migrationlawyerperthwa.com.au/blog/subclass-601-eta-visa-eligibility-criteria-and-visa-conditions/ (2026-06-27 revalidation - ETA 601 eligible nationalities confirmed unchanged)
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visa_policy_of_Australia (2026-06-27 revalidation cross-reference - no policy changes in 2025-26 noted)
https://library.fairwork.gov.au/viewer/?krn=K600486 (2026-06-29 - FWIT table: AUD 183,100 for 2025-26, July 2026 figure not yet on this page)
https://ternvisa.com/en/blog/eta-visa-australia-guide (2026-06-29 revalidation - ETA 601 and eVisitor 651 eligible country lists confirmed unchanged as of May 2026 update)

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa (subclass 600)
visa_types.purpose: Tourism, visiting family, business visitor activities
visa_types.notes: Applied for online via ImmiAccount. Stay is typically up to 3 months per visit. Some applicants may be granted up to 12 months. Fee is approximately AUD 190.

visa_types.name: Student Visa (subclass 500)
visa_types.purpose: Full-time study in a registered course
visa_types.notes: Requires a Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) from a CRICOS-registered institution. Can work up to 48 hours per fortnight. Fee approximately AUD 650.

visa_types.name: Temporary Skill Shortage Visa (subclass 482)
visa_types.purpose: Working in an approved occupation for an approved sponsor
visa_types.notes: Requires an Australian sponsor. Short-term stream (2 years) and medium-term stream (4 years) available. Fee approximately AUD 1,455–3,115 depending on stream.

visa_types.name: Working Holiday Visa (subclass 417 / 462)
visa_types.purpose: Holiday and short-term work (age 18–30/35 for select nationalities)
visa_types.notes: 417 is for citizens of Ireland, UK, Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, South Korea, and others. 462 is for USA, Thailand, China and others. Can be extended twice to 3 years total by doing regional work. Fee approximately AUD 510.

visa_types.name: Investor Retirement Visa (subclass 405) - Note: closed to new applicants
visa_types.purpose: Retired investors (no longer available for new applicants since 2018)
visa_types.notes: Closed to new applications. Existing visa holders may remain.
